title: Level 313
wikitext:
{{Rewrite|1=Frame one is a bit of an overlill, and i dont get how it comes into the level.|date=June 27, 2026}} {{Audio|file=Shardbound OST REVERBERATION OF THE CRIMSON DREAM Normal Theme-SlowedAndReverbLite (mp3cut.net).wav|volume=1|=yes|loop=yes|secrettext=psst}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 313: 'Melodic Ministry'}} {{Blurquote|image=Nottriinsteadmilky.jpg|quote-text=
John? Where are you? Hello? G-Gosh... I can't see anything down there. W-whatever, let me just find a way out...

|person=Sarah}} {{Blurbackground|file=GvOl0eyWwAAibUN.jpg|opacity=0.6}} {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inconsolata:wght@500&family=Quicksand:wght@300..700&display=swap'); body { font-family: "Trebuchet MS", Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif !important; color: #ffffff !important; /* Removed background-color definition here */ } /* These containers will sit on top of the blurred background */ .page__main, .main-container { background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.85) !important; /* Semi-transparent black for content area */ border: 2px solid #ffffff !important; } .wiki-page-content, .article-content, .mw-parser-output { background-color: transparent !important; /* Keep the wiki content transparent so the semi-transparent container color shows through */ } .rail-module, .border-box, .right-rail-wrapper { background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 1) !important; /* Set rail background to semi-transparent black */ border-color: #000000 !important; border: 0px solid #ffffff !important; /* Ensures the border is visible */ } .fandom-container { background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 1) !important; border: 3px solid #8A2BE2 !important; box-shadow: 0 0 15px #8A2BE2, 0 0 25px #8A2BE2, 0 0 40px rgba(138, 43, 226, 0.5); padding: 20px; margin: 20px; border-radius: 15px; } h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, p, li, span { color: #ffffff !important; } a { color: #B0AFFF !important; } }} '''Level 313''' is the '''314th''' level of the [[Backrooms Wiki|Backrooms]].

{{Alt Class|ent=0|env=5|ext=0|no=313|noname=1}}

{{Blurimage|file=1980s-old-positive-film-scanned-interior-saint-brigids-cathedral-market-square-kildare-ireland.webp|caption=A separate room from the main platform.}} {{Blurimage|file=Something I found.webp|caption=A model of "Frame One".}}'''Level 313''', colloquially known as "'''Melodic Ministry'''" or "'''The Ministry'''," is a vast series of dilapidated Gothic Cathedrals suspended over a seemingly infinite void. It has no conventional floor due to the entire level being elevated above the abyss below. The chasm over which the cathedral is situated bears striking similarities to [[The Void]]. However, it remains undetermined whether '''Level 313''' is located within [[The Void]], or if it exists within its own separate void. '''Level 313''''s primary dangers come from architectural decay, extreme conditions (e.g., "Frame One") and environmental degradation. Structural failure generally occurs when the weight exceeds ''225 lbs (102.1 kg)''; despite this, the level suffers from [[Phenomenon 15]], making such collapses extremely improbable. Additionally, '''Level 313''' contains nonsensical topography (e.g., random holes in the ground, turns that lead into a wall, an incline that leads one to [[The Void]], etc). This nonsensicality primarily occurs on Frame One, occurring less frequently in other sections of the level (e.g., Cathedrals). '''Level 313''' is completely devoid of entities. Conversely, [[Phenomenon 35]] can still occur within this level. Following the conclusion of The Wretched Cycle, due to [[Phenomenon 15]], Wretches are completely alone. As a result, most perish due to the absence of sustenance. ==
Frame One
== '''Frame One''', colloquially known as '''"Pathways,"''' is an extremely narrow platform that serves as the structural foundation for all subsequent sections of the level. It manifests as a thin walkway with continuous, sharp turns, extending infinitely wherever it deviates. Near a structure, such as a Cathedral, '''Frame One''' develops fences on both sides of the surface. This ostensibly functions as a barrier to prevent falls once an explorer reaches a structure. Alternatively, these fences can exist to prevent a wanderer from reaching a Fountain of Blessing unless they were to climb over the barrier. The ambient temperature of '''Frame One''' is a staggering '''-182°C (-295.6°F).''' At this temperature, human tissue freezes instantly. This causes 4th-degree frostbite, in addition to resulting conditions such as necrosis (water expanding in cells to form ice crystals, causing permanent cellular death), making traveling on '''Frame One''' nearly impossible unless one comes into contact with a Fountain of Blessing. Alternatively, a wanderer needs suits typically used in Aerospace Engineering (e.g., Aerospace Cryogenic Suits, Multi-Layer Insulation) to survive. Additionally, oxygen condenses into a liquid at '''-182.96°C (-297.33°F)'''. At only '''0.96°C''' above its boiling point, oxygen gas is extremely dense and can behave like a heavy mist or fog as it begins to settle. Consequently, touching a surface that is at or below its boiling point causes the gas to instantly condense into liquid form, appearing as oxygen dew. Atypically, if '''Frame One's''' path deviates upwards from its baseline elevation, the temperature rapidly increases per every 2 inches. Conversely, if '''Frame One's''' path deviates downwards, the temperature will remain constant. It is currently unknown why this occurs. Nevertheless, it is presumed to relate to the confirmed exit to [[Level 993]]; specifically, this shift occurs to align with the average temperature of [[Level 993]]. ==
Structures and Phenomena
== |-| Cathedrals & Fountain of Blessings= Cathedrals are the most common structure within '''Level 313''', which is the main point of entry to this level. Manifested in a compromised or substandard state, they are functionally obsolescent. As with the rest of '''Level 313''', Cathedrals are also affected by the '''blue shift''' phenomenon. Cathedrals take the appearance of a typical cathedral, containing '''The Nave''', '''The Transept''', and '''The Crossing'''. The overhead structure consists of ribbed vaulting that extends to extreme altitudes, often leading to structural failure, collapse, or fragility. As such, most Cathedrals are destroyed. {{Blurimage|file=Southeastern_side_altar_at_St_Mary's_Cathedral,_Sydney.jpg|caption=An edited image of a Cathedral to remove the blue-shifting.}} Additionally, most Cathedrals emanate perpetual hymnal music regardless of the presence of a source, or the state of the structure's integrity. Upon exiting Cathedrals, the sound of said hymnal music instantaneously dissipates, as opposed to fading out. Furthermore, any active instrument causes the ambient music to cease gradually, as it takes precedence over the ambiance. Whilst the cause is unknown, this phenomenon presumably prioritizes the wanderer's input. '''Fountain of Blessings''' are more common in Cathedrals than in the open. Oddly, the ambient hymnal music changes to a more energetic state, resembling "'''The Vamp'''", as one approaches a '''Fountain of Blessing'''. Presumably, using a '''Fountain of Blessing''' is a religious process. Its physical structure takes the appearance of a standard water fountain, with an oleaginous, xanthic liquid. These structures are anomalous in nature and exhibit random effects upon consumption. Notably, the effects placed upon an explorer once said liquid is consumed stays permanently. Effects of this liquid include: extreme knowledge (e.g., general mathematics, exceedingly easier no-clipping, etc), the extreme deficit of knowledge (e.g., how to breathe, how to walk, the inability to eat, etc) and other undocumented effects. The liquid only applies such effects in large quantities. |-| Blue Shifting= Blue Shifting is a phenomenon that affects the entire level, rendering all physical objects a monochromatic blue. Though the underlying mechanism remains unidentified, there are multiple theories that could explain why the phenomenon occurs. ==
The Freefall Theory
== ''This theory supports the "Separate Void" theory. '' '''The Freefall Theory''' suggests that '''Level 313''' is not stationary, instead, '''Level 313''' in constant acceleration due to an unknown gravitational force, moving at relativistic speeds. As it travels through its void, it causes all incoming light and matter to shift towards the high-frequency end of the visual spectrum. This object could be stellar objects, such as a Black Hole, similar to or larger than [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/TON_618 TON 618] or [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Phoenix_Cluster Phoenix A]; alternatively, '''Level 313''' has enough mass to reach a terminal velocity that is sub-relativistic to relativistic, and is currently accelerating to that point. However, this theory conflicts with the exit to [[Level 993]]. Due to the temperature rising per every two inches ascended, the wanderer would still be in a state of free fall, and would not truly be ascending. Moreover, the notion that when the wanderer descends, the temperature remains constant, further supports the "Typical Phenomena" theory, violating the laws of thermodynamics. This theory is supported by slightly more than the "Typical Phenomena" theory. ==
The Contraction Theory
== ''This theory supports the "Separate Void" theory. '' '''The Contraction Theory''' suggests that the void '''Level 313''' resides in is finite, and is currently contracting. This theory is the most recent of the three; in addition to having limited evidence. This theory is supported by very few. ==
The Typical Phenomena Theory
== ''This theory supports the, "[[The Void]]" theory. '' '''The Typical Phenomena Theory''' suggests that similar to other phenomena (e.g., [[Phenomenon 15]], [[Phenomenon 12]]), the blue shifting is caused by an unknown source, originating from the backrooms. Multiple sub-theories have been created from this theory; though many have been disproven (e.g., an assumption that the "Typical Phenomena" is purely visual to the wanderer. Disproven due to photography also capturing blue-shifted light). This theory is supported by few. ==
Logs
==
Phone Recording
''Found presumably from '''Sarah's''' phone.''
"Ugh... it won't work here either." "Why do I always expect signal? I'm just... stupid." "Whatever... at least this cathedral looks, uh... beautiful. It won't just freeze my hand off." "..." ''She sighs.'' "I don't know what the hell is even going on. I'm sleeping, suddenly I wake up in some [[Level 1|gray parking lot]], and then '''John''' and '''Kio''' were there..." "At first, I just thought this was some fever dream. Sadly, it's not." "I still can't find normal water. I'm stuck drinking some [[Object 1|water tasting almonds]], wait... no, almond tasting water. I can't even speak straight, god..." ''She pauses for an upwards of 5 minutes.'' "On the positive side, at least this is a place he can't [[Level 612|burn to a crisp]]. 'I don't like it, so I'm burning it.' I don't know why I still search for that idiot." "...but I still don't wanna just... let him die." "Doesn't fit me." "...the real question is how the actual hell do I get out of here. If I step out, I freeze. There's no food here... no water..." "Maybe- Oh. I'm recording. Well... I don't wanna waste battery, now do I..." End of Log
Notebook
''Found from a torn piece of paper on a seat in the '''Cathedrals'''.''
"So Frigid." "Just so, so Frigid." End of Log
== Entities == Due to [[Phenomenon 15]], entities pose no threat to wanderers who enter this level. == Colonies and Outposts == Due to the nature of Level 313, it is near-impossible to find stable ground to construct an outpost or colony. == Entrances == There is only one confirmed entrance and multiple unconfirmed entrances to '''Level 313'''. * Falling in [[The Void]], one may eventually come across the level, though unconfirmed. * Attempting to no-clip to [[The Void]] presumably has rare chance to lead to [[Level 313]], though unconfirmed. * Entering a deteriorated church in [[Level 112]] may bring one to the level. This method of entry is confirmed, but is not guaranteed to succeed if attempted. == Exits == Level 313 has many guaranteed exits; however, these exits are often unsafe. * Jumping off the platform presumably leads to [[The Void]], or an alternative version of it. * Exiting the way one entered sends one to a [https://backrooms.fandom.com/wiki/Special:RandomInCategory/Levels|Random Random Level]. * Ascending for an undetermined amount of time sends one to [[Level 993]]. * Breaking a glass window in a cathedral sends one to [[Level 612]]. {{AuthorInformation|author1=[[User:TerriusTranquility|Terrius]]|message= Thanks for reading! ==
Pages by Terrius
== === [[Level 997]] === "Level 997 is a vast system of mechanical and biological phenomena. Wanderers who were able to travel through the entirety of the level have reported seeing a vast network of coexisting systems. Each system is accessible by one another. These systems are centered around the mega structure, The Clockwork." === [[Level 414]] === "Level 414, also known as "SunnySide," is a sprawling expanse with varying environments. Wanderers who stayed within Sunnyside for extended periods of time report constant cases of Mental Disconnection." === [[Level 612]] === "Level 612 manifests as a room littered with flowers and cakes, with nobody around; celebratory music playing regardless of where the wanderer is located." === [[Level 526]] === "Level 526, commonly referred to as "All Theatres Are Haunted," is an interlinked network of historic theatres, each resembling 19th and early 20th-century venues." btw, there's a rule for this: "Cathedrals", specfically the plural kind, reference the structure in the level, "cathedral" references the real world cathedrals. === Credits are below: === The song that plays is created by me; for a scrapped project. Full Song: [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NBuHPpZuQfU&list=PLfNy7ORLZyC3ZxTgOFCxJr6KbwLeG0hCq&index=13 Reverberation of the Crimson Dream] You can use my songs, just make sure to credit me! ==

I searched for around 3 hours on 1/24/2026, going to major 3d model sites(Sketchfab) and I could not find it. Thus, it classifies as Orphan work. Still, if the creator desires for it to be taken down, please contact me, as I do not own the image. ==
